title: Walmart Store Sales
description: This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010  and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of  the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.

Walmart Store Sales
This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010 and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.
Step Name:Import CSV file into Power BI Desktop
Detailed Instructions:

Steps

Download the 'Walmart Sales Data'  file by clicking the red icon above.
Open Power BI Desktop
Click on Get data button 
Select Text/CSV and click on connect 
Select the 'Walmart' CSV file and click on Open
A preview of the data will be displayed, click on Load 

Insight: 

Importing Bulk Files in Power BI

Walmart Store Sales
This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010 and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.
Step Name:Create Calendar Table, and Refresh Date Table
Detailed Instructions:
Steps(Calendar Table)
On Power BI desktop under Modeling click on New table button 

Type the Dax formula below in the Dax formula bar, Click on the check mark, and save

Calendar = ADDCOLUMNS(CALENDARAUTO(),

"Year", Year([Date]),

"Month Number", Month([Date]),

"Month Name Short", FORMAT([Date],"MMM")

)

Steps(Refresh Date Table)

On Power BI Desktop ribbon, Click on Transform data
Click on New Source button, and select Blank query from the list and click on Connect
Under properties in the Name box type in 'Refresh Date'
On the Dax formula bar type ' =Date Time.LocalNow()'
Click on Convert to Table button 
After the refresh table pops up, rename the column by right clicking and choosing rename from the selection
Click  on Close & apply button and save 

Insight: 

How to create a Date Table in Power BI

Walmart Store Sales
This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010 and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.
Step Name:Create a relationship between Walmart Table and Calendar Table
Detailed Instructions:

Steps

Go to the data modeling page by clicking on this icon --> 
Drag and connect the Date column of the Walmart table with that of the Calendar Table

Insight: 
Understanding Relationships in Power BI Desktop

Walmart Store Sales
This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010 and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.
Step Name:Create conditional columns
Detailed Instructions:

Steps(Holiday Weeks)

Select Transform Data from the Home tab
Under power query Editor click on Add Column tab
Select Conditional Column 
In New Column Name box type in 'Holiday Flag 2'
In the IF statement boxes type in values like shown in the image below
After filling up one line to go to the next line, click on the Add Clause button 
Press OK
Under Home tab , Click close and Apply  

Steps(Unemployment Rate)

Select Transform Data from the Home tab
Under power query Editor click on Add Column tab
Select Conditional Column 
In New Column Name box type in 'Unemployment Rate'
In the IF statement boxes type in values like shown in the image below
After filling up one line, to go to the next line click on the Add Clause button 
After filling up all the statements needed, Press OK
Under Home tab , Click close and Apply  

Insight: 
Writing IF Statements in Power Query

Walmart Store Sales
This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010 and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.
Step Name:Create DAX measures
Detailed Instructions:

Steps 

Select the Walmart Table under the fields pane by clicking on it 
From Power BI desktop ribbon, Home tab click on New measure 
The formula bar appears along the top of the report canvas. 
 Fill in all the DAX formulas provided below, one at a time, and when you finish Press Enter or select Commit (checkmark icon) in the formula bar.  

After creating each measure choose the necessary format and decimal for each value (measure)
Save

DAX Formulas

Total Sales = SUM(Walmart[Weekly_Sales])

 

Sales Per Month = DIVIDE([Total Sales],DISTINCTCOUNT('Calendar'[Month Name Short]),0)+0

 

AVG Sales = AVERAGE(Walmart[Weekly_Sales])

 

Sales YOY =

VAR PYSales =

CALCULATE([AVG Sales], DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,YEAR))

RETURN

IF(ISBLANK(PYSales),0,DIVIDE(([AVG Sales]-PYSales),PYSales))          

 

AVG CPI = AVERAGE(Walmart[CPI])

 

CPI YOY =

VAR LYCPI =

CALCULATE([AVG CPI],DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,YEAR))

RETURN

IF(ISBLANK(LYCPI),0,DIVIDE(([AVG CPI]-LYCPI),LYCPI))  

 

Arrow YOY% = SWITCH(TRUE(), [.Sales YOY] <0, UNICHAR(9662),[.Sales YOY] =0, UNICHAR(9666), UNICHAR(9652))

Arrow YOY CPI = SWITCH(TRUE(), [.CPI YOY] <0, UNICHAR(9662),[.CPI YOY] =0, UNICHAR(9666), UNICHAR(9652))

Formats

Total Sales = Currency, Decimals = 2
Sales per Month = Currency, Decimals = 2
AVG Sales = Currency, Decimals = 2
Sales YOY = Percentage, Decimals = 2
AVG CPI = Currency, Decimals = 2
CPI YOY = Percentage, Decimals = 2
Arrow YOY% = Text
Arrow YOY CPI = Text

Insight: 
DAX Formulas & Creating Measures

Walmart Store Sales
This project dashboard assess year over year sales conducted among 45 random Walmart Stores in the US between Feb 2010 and Oct 2012. Although this was a dawn era of coming out of the great recession of 2008, these Walmart Stores have faced an impact in sales like any discount or department stores.
Step Name:Adding Project Logos and Title for the Dashboard
Detailed Instructions:

Steps (Colaberry Logo)

From Power BI Desktop ribbon under Insert, click on Image from Elements
Select the colaberry logo from the downloadable file provided in this project
Under the Visualizations pane , click on General and fill in the figures provided below

          Size: Height=73, Width=205
          Position: Horizontal=0, Vertical=7

Steps (Walmart Logo)

From Power BI Desktop ribbon under Insert, click on Image from Elements
Select the Walmart logo from the downloadable file provided in this project
Under the Visualizations pane, Image, Style, and Scaling select the Fill option from the dropdown 

 Click on General and fill in the figures provided below

          Size: Height=73, Width=205
          Position: Horizontal=1075, Vertical=8

Steps (Title)

From Power BI Desktop ribbon under Insert, click on Text box from Elements
Type in 'Walmart Store Sales (Feb 2010 - Oct 2012)' in the text box
Select center alignment for the text (Font = Segoe(bold), Font size = 28, Font Color = #4184C5) 
 Click on General and under properties, fill in the figures provided below
          Size: Height=73, Width=1279
          Position: Horizontal=1, Vertical=7
Click on Format from Power Bi Desktop, Select Send Backward tab, and click on Send to back button

 

Save after you're done 

Insight: 
Using IMAGES in Power BI

Step Name:Line and Stacked Column Visuals
Detailed Instructions:

Line and Stacked Column Visual 1(Sales by Month)

Steps

Click on an empty space on the canvas, and from the visualizations pane select Line and Stacked Column Chart
Fill up figures provided below for the necessary fields

Build Visual Tab:

Shared axis = Year, Month Name Short
Column Values = Sales per Month
Line Values = Sales YOY, CPI YOY
Tooltips = Sales YOY, CPI, CPI YOY
After filling up the above rename fields, as per gif provided below
Sort axis by Month Name Short, and ascending order

Format Visual Tab:

General: Properties, Size, Height=310, Width=557, Position, Horizontal=206, Vertical=80
               Title=On, Text=Sales by Month, Font=Segoe UI Bold (15), Text color=#FFFFFF, Background color=#4184C5, Horizontal alignment=Center
               Effects: Background=On, Background color=#E6E6E6, Visual boarder=On, Boarder color-#CCCCCC 

Visual: X-axis: Values: Font=Segoe UI (9), Font color=#333333
                         Title = Off

             Y-axis: Values: Font=Segoe UI Bold (9), Font color=#00498D, Display Units=Millions
                         Title = Off

             Secondary Y-axis: Range: Align Zeros = On
                                           Values: Font=Segoe UI Bold (9), Font color=#D9B300, Display Units=Auto
                                           Title = Off

             Gridlines: Horizontal=On, Color=#333333

             Lines: Colors: Sales YOY=D9B300, CPI YOY=#9BA5A8, Shade area=On

             Markers= On

             Columns: Colors=00498D

             Data labels: Sales YOY = Off
                                 CPI YOY = Off  
                                 Sales per Mth: Position(Column)= Inside end, Orientation (Column)= Vertical
                                                         Values: Font=Segoe UI (9), Color=#FFFFFF, Value decimal places= 0
                                                          Background = Off

portfolioContent.title: Walmart Store Sales
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a Power BI dashboard analyzing year-over-year sales trends across 45 Walmart stores post-2008 recession.
portfolioContent.summary: This project involved creating a comprehensive Power BI dashboard to analyze year-over-year sales data from 45 Walmart stores in the US between 2010 and 2012. It focused on visualizing sales trends during the recovery period following the 2008 recession. The dashboard incorporated DAX measures, calendar tables, and conditional columns to provide actionable retail insights.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: Walmart stores, like other discount retailers, experienced sales fluctuations during the recovery from the 2008 recession. Understanding these sales trends was critical to identifying performance drivers and areas needing strategic focus. The challenge was to analyze multi-year sales data effectively to support data-driven decision making.
objectives: Analyze year-over-year sales trends across multiple Walmart stores
Create an interactive Power BI dashboard for retail sales visualization
Incorporate economic and seasonal factors such as holidays and unemployment rates
workflow: Import Walmart sales CSV data into Power BI Desktop
Create calendar and refresh date tables using DAX
Establish relationships between sales and calendar tables
Add conditional columns for holiday flags and unemployment rates
Develop DAX measures for total sales, average sales, and year-over-year growth
keyInsights: Sales trends reflected gradual recovery post-2008 recession across stores
Holiday weeks influenced sales performance significantly
Unemployment rate variations correlated with sales fluctuations
Year-over-year sales growth varied by store and month
businessImpact: Enabled Walmart to identify sales recovery patterns and seasonality effects
Supported strategic planning with data-driven insights on store performance
Improved retail decision-making through interactive and dynamic dashboards
